description: Classify common and preferred equity variants — voting, participating, cumulative, convertible, callable, putable — and capture their cash-flow and control rights

fields.name: features
fields.label: voting | cumulative | participating | convertible | callable | putable

common_shares.residual_claim: Last in line for assets and earnings
common_shares.voting_rights: One share one vote (typical); may have dual-class
common_shares.dividends: Discretionary, paid after debt and preferred

preferred_shares.cumulative: Unpaid dividends accumulate; must be paid before common dividends
preferred_shares.participating: Receive extra dividends when common exceeds threshold
preferred_shares.convertible: Convert to common at pre-set ratio
preferred_shares.callable: Issuer can redeem at call price

voting_variants.statutory: One vote per share per director
voting_variants.cumulative: Votes can be concentrated on one nominee

non_domestic_access.direct_investing: Buy on foreign exchange; FX and settlement risk

depository_receipts.sponsored_adr: Issuer-backed ADR; levels I-III increase disclosure
depository_receipts.gdr: Global DR, often traded in London/Luxembourg

risk_return.risk: Standard deviation of returns; preferred less volatile than common
risk_return.return: Price change plus dividends; currency effects for ADR/GDR

classify_equity_security.description: Record classification and features of an equity security

errors.message: equity_type must be common or preferred

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat preferred dividends as risk-free
anti_patterns.why: Preferreds rank above common but below debt; non-cumulative issues can skip dividends without default

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore dual-class voting in governance models
anti_patterns.why: Founders with super-voting retain control indefinitely; minority shareholders have limited influence

anti_patterns.rule: Do not compare ADR returns without adjusting for FX and reference asset
anti_patterns.why: ADR price reflects underlying plus currency; performance attribution requires both components
